title: DepartureAttestation (v1)
description: Source-relay-signed snapshot of an agent's history at the relay being departed. Lets destination relays understand the agent's trust level, task counts, and economic state without trusting the agent's self-report. See spec/migration-v1.md §5.1.

motebit_id.description: Agent's motebit identity being attested for.

first_seen.description: Unix timestamp in milliseconds when the agent first registered at this relay.

last_active.description: Unix timestamp in milliseconds of the agent's last task or interaction.

trust_level.description: Agent's trust level at departure. Free-form string per spec; conventional values include `untrusted`, `verified`, `trusted`, `revoked` — the destination relay applies its own trust mapping.

successful_tasks.description: Total completed tasks the agent executed as worker (≥ 0).

failed_tasks.description: Total failed tasks the agent executed as worker (≥ 0).

credentials_issued.description: Total credentials this relay issued to the agent (≥ 0).

balance_at_departure.description: Virtual account balance in micro-units (1 USD = 1,000,000) at attestation time. Lets destination relays understand the economic state the agent is bringing across.

attested_at.description: Unix timestamp in milliseconds when this attestation was signed.

suite.description: Cryptosuite identifier. Always `motebit-jcs-ed25519-b64-v1` for migration artifacts: JCS canonicalization (RFC 8785), Ed25519 primitive, base64url signature, hex public key. Verifiers reject missing or unknown values fail-closed.

signature.description: Base64url-encoded Ed25519 signature over `canonicalJson(body)` where `body` = the artifact minus `signature`. Signed by the source relay. Foundation law (§5.3): the relay MUST NOT fabricate or inflate attestation data.
